content: We can model the real-world impact of any power-exerting agent as a sigmoid activation curve: an agent’s incremental efforts produce little measurable effect at first, then rapidly shift from negligible to full impact once a threshold of accumulated power or action is crossed, before plateauing at the maximum possible effect the agent can exert. This framing lets us quantify and predict when an agent’s actions will cross from non-impactful to transformative in domains from pathogen spread to policy advocacy.
